response.justification: The candidate is a strong, versatile senior engineer, but their profile does not match this role's core requirements. Professional C++ experience is limited to roughly seven months at National Instruments in 2014–2015 plus a thesis project, far short of the mandatory 5–10 years of hands-on C++ on Windows. There is no fixed-income, derivatives, or capital-markets systems experience — the closest is a fintech-adjacent stint at RIDE Capital, which was TypeScript/Node work. SQL exposure appears general (MySQL/MongoDB) rather than deep SQL Server/Oracle work.
strengths: Broad, senior-level engineering background across web, embedded, and low-latency systems (DLang async I/O at millions of requests/sec)
Strong software craftsmanship practices: TDD, CI/CD, pairing, trunk-based development
Excellent communication skills demonstrated through workshops, conference talks, and teaching
concerns: C++ experience is minimal and dated (~7 months professionally, ended 2015), versus the mandatory 5–10 years on Windows
No fixed-income, capital-markets, or investment-banking domain experience
No SQL Server or Oracle experience listed; database background is MySQL/MongoDB-centric

key_factors.factor: Strong general engineering fundamentals and communication
